laws.title: New Jersey Minimum Wage — $15.49/hr (2024)
laws.summary: New Jersey's minimum wage is $15.49/hour for most employees (2024). Small employers (under 6 employees) and seasonal employers: $13.73/hr. Agricultural workers: $12.81/hr. New Jersey reached $15/hr for large employers in 2023 and continues to adjust for inflation annually.
compliance_actions: Ensure all New Jersey employees are paid at least $15.49/hr (large employers)
Post the New Jersey minimum wage notice at all NJ worksites
Monitor for the annual January 1 adjustment each year

laws.title: New Jersey WARN Act — Expanded Notice Requirements
laws.summary: New Jersey significantly expanded its WARN Act, requiring 90 days' notice (vs. federal 60 days) for mass layoffs affecting 50+ full-time employees. New Jersey also requires severance pay equal to 1 week per year of service when proper notice is not given. This is broader than the federal WARN Act and can result in significant financial liability.
compliance_actions: If planning a reduction in force affecting NJ employees: give 90 days' written notice to affected employees, the NJ Dept of Labor, and local chief elected official
Failure to provide full notice triggers mandatory severance of 1 week per year of service
Consult employment counsel before any layoffs involving New Jersey employees
